skill.name: azure-postgres
skill.description: Configure Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with Microsoft Entra ID authentication, managed identities, group access, and migration guidance for passwordless database access.
skill.summary: Configure Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server for passwordless Microsoft Entra ID access.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis raised many command, filesystem, credential, and network alerts. Most Markdown backtick and documentation alerts are false positives, but runnable shell scripts perform privileged Azure and PostgreSQL changes and interpolate user-controlled values into SQL without robust escaping. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should wait for script hardening.

high_findings.title: SQL Injection Risk in Privileged PostgreSQL Setup Scripts
high_findings.description: The setup scripts pass user-controlled user names, group names, identity names, role names, and database names into psql command strings and GRANT statements without robust SQL literal and identifier escaping. These commands run as an Entra PostgreSQL administrator, so maliciously crafted input could alter database roles or permissions beyond the requested setup.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly concatenate shell arguments and Azure-derived display names into SQL executed by psql. The context confirms privileged database role and permission changes, making the injection impact concrete.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Azure and PostgreSQL Mutations Require Manual Review
medium_findings.description: The skill includes runnable shell scripts and command references that enable Entra authentication, add administrators, grant database permissions, enable group sync, and disable password authentication. These operations are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they have high operational impact if copied or run against the wrong resource.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit Azure CLI and psql administrative operations. They match the skill objective, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Short-Lived Access Tokens Exported to Environment Variables
medium_findings.description: Several scripts export Azure PostgreSQL access tokens into PGPASSWORD for psql connections. This is a common PostgreSQL pattern, but environment variables can be exposed to local processes or logs in some execution environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token export is directly visible in multiple scripts. The token is short-lived and intended for psql, so the issue is moderate exposure risk rather than credential theft evidence.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: Many reported external command, filesystem, weak cryptography, and Windows SAM alerts are Markdown examples, relative links, SQL boolean values, or PostgreSQL password-management text. They do not indicate Ruby execution, path traversal, weak hashing, or Windows credential access in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is in Markdown code fences, links, or SQL examples and is not executable by the skill itself. File context shows documentation rather than hidden runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Azure Portal URLs Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Azure Portal resource pages and are presented as user-facing link templates. No evidence found of outbound network exfiltration or calls to unknown endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static portal examples inside documentation. There is no code path that sends local data to those URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: psql SQL Built from Unescaped Variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts build SQL statements with variables inside quoted strings and heredocs instead of parameterized SQL or strict identifier validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in runnable shell scripts and affects administrator SQL statements. It is the strongest confirmed issue in the audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution for Cloud Identity Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts use shell command substitution to retrieve Azure identity data, server names, and access tokens before using those values in later commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution is expected in Azure automation, and most shell arguments are quoted. The risk comes from later trust in returned values, not from command substitution alone.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authentication Mode Changes Can Lock Out Password Users
dangerous_patterns.description: The command references include disabling password authentication after Entra migration. This is legitimate guidance, but it can cause outage or lockout if administrators run it before validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly set password authentication to disabled. The behavior is intended but operationally dangerous without migration checks.

content.value_statement: Azure PostgreSQL authentication is difficult to configure safely across users, groups, and applications. This skill provides focused Entra ID guidance, scripts, and troubleshooting steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides Microsoft Entra ID authentication setup for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Provides scripts for user, group, managed identity, and migration workflows.
Explains PostgreSQL role mapping with pgaadauth functions and security labels.
Includes permission templates for read-only, read-write, and administrator access.
Documents troubleshooting steps for token, role, firewall, and username issues.
References Azure MCP tools and Azure CLI fallback commands.
limitations: Requires Azure CLI, psql, jq, and appropriate Azure permissions for script use.
Does not validate every database, tenant, or network policy before changes.
Scripts should be reviewed and hardened before production automation.
Focused on Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server, not self-managed PostgreSQL.

use_cases.title: Migrate Production Access to Entra ID
use_cases.description: Plan and execute a staged move from passwords to Entra ID authentication for existing PostgreSQL roles.

use_cases.title: Grant Developer Database Access
use_cases.description: Create Entra-mapped PostgreSQL roles and assign least-privilege permissions for individual developers.

use_cases.title: Connect Azure Apps Without Secrets
use_cases.description: Configure managed identities so hosted applications can connect to PostgreSQL without stored passwords.

prompt_templates.title: Check Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server setup and list what I need before enabling Microsoft Entra ID authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Add Developer Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me grant read-only Entra ID access to a developer for one Azure PostgreSQL database. Include validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Managed Identity
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a passwordless access plan for an Azure-hosted app using managed identity and Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Password Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe migration from password authentication to Entra-only authentication for existing PostgreSQL roles and applications.

output_examples.input: I need to add a developer with read-only access.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering Entra admin verification, role creation, read-only grants, token-based connection testing, and rollback considerations.

output_examples.input: My app should connect without a stored database password.
output_examples.output: A managed identity setup plan with required Azure identity details, PostgreSQL role mapping, permission scope, and SDK connection guidance.

output_examples.input: We want to disable password authentication.
output_examples.output: A migration sequence that maps existing roles, tests Entra logins, disables old passwords, and changes authentication mode after validation.

best_practices: Test Entra authentication for every role before disabling password authentication.
Use least-privilege grants and prefer group-based access for teams.
Review generated shell commands before running them against production resources.
anti_patterns: Do not grant administrator permissions to applications that only need data access.
Do not store PostgreSQL access tokens or database passwords in source control.
Do not switch to Entra-only authentication before validating all users and applications.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure PostgreSQL servers?
faq.answer: It includes basic server command references, but its main focus is authentication and access configuration for Flexible Server.

faq.question: Can it help with managed identities?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains managed identity role mapping and includes a script for application access setup.

faq.question: Does it support group-based database access?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Entra group roles, optional group sync, and group permission grants.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. Review and adapt the scripts first because they perform privileged Azure and PostgreSQL changes.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: The scripts expect Azure CLI, psql, jq, and an account with suitable Azure and PostgreSQL administrator permissions.

faq.question: Does it replace Azure documentation?
faq.answer: No. It provides focused workflow guidance and examples that should be checked against current Azure documentation.
